candidates.why: Operating D&A summary row above the financial section, with component depreciation/amortization rows beneath it. However, it is not labelled as COGS/cost of sales and appears to be total operating D&A, so attributing it specifically to the selected COGS bucket ('Use of materials and services') is uncertain.

Operating depreciation component disclosed separately, but not identified as COGS-specific and appears part of the overall D&A block.
Operating amortization component within the same D&A block, but not labelled as belonging to cost of sales.

variables.notes: The selected COGS bucket is only 'Use of materials and services'. A separate operating D&A block exists at the same level, so D&A does not appear embedded in that selected COGS row. But the disclosed D&A rows are not labelled as COGS/cost of sales, so assigning any of them specifically to COGS would be speculative. Conservative output avoids double counting.

candidates.why: Best summary candidate for separate operating D&A because it sits as its own operating line above EBIT and appears to summarize the component D&A rows below. It is outside the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS rows, so if any D&A is to be added separately later, this summary row is the preferred non-duplicative choice.

variables.notes: A distinct operating D&A summary row is disclosed separately from the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS rows ('other Selling, General & Administration', 'Employee benefit expenses', 'Other Operating Expenses', 'Share-based incentive scheme'), so it does not appear embedded in those selected rows. The summary row is preferred over its component depreciation/amortization lines to avoid double counting. Manual review is flagged because the statement does not explicitly label this D&A as SG&A-specific; it may represent total operating D&A.
